Items.Title: Petitions and Communications
Land Use Call-Ups

By Council Member Farías:
Pursuant to Rule 11.20(b) of the Council Rules and Section 19-160.2 of the New York City Administrative Code, the Council resolves that the action of the Department of Transportation approving a sidewalk café located at 1200 Castle Hill Avenue, Bronx, NY 10462, Borough of the Bronx, Council District 18, Community District 9, related to Application No. D 2650051365 SWX (Sabrosura) shall be subject to review by the Council.
Items.ActionText: A motion was made that this Land Use Call-Up be Approved, by Council approved by consent Roll Call.
Items.MatterName: Zoning, Sabrosura, Bronx (D 2650051365 SWX).

Items.Title:
By Council Member Mealy:
Pursuant to Rule 11.20(b) of the Council Rules and Section 19-160.2 of the New York City Administrative Code, the Council resolves that the action of the Department of Transportation approving a sidewalk café located at 185 Howard Avenue, Brooklyn, NY 11233, Borough of Brooklyn, Council District 41, Community District 3, related to Application No. D 2650179761 SWK (Cicchetti BK) shall be subject to review by the Council.
Items.ActionText: A motion was made that this Land Use Call-Up be Approved, by Council approved by consent Roll Call.
Items.MatterName: Zoning, Cicchetti BK, Brooklyn (D 2650179761 SWK).

Items.Title: Rules, Privileges, Elections, Standards and Elections
Items.AgendaNote: RULES, PRIVILEGES, ELECTIONS, STANDARDS AND ELECTIONS

Items.Title: Resolution declaring March 24 annually as Women of Color in Tech Day in the City of New York to recognize the contributions of women of color to the technology industry and to acknowledge the challenges they still face
Items.AgendaNote: Amended and Adopted by the Committee on Women and Gender Equity
Items.MinutesNote:
Pursuant to Rule 8.60 of the Council, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) called for a voice vote.  Hearing those in favor, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) declared the Resolution to be adopted.

Adopted by the Council by voice-vote.
Items.ActionText: A motion was made that this Resolution be Approved, by Council approved by voice vote.
Items.MatterName: Declaring March 24 annually as Women of Color in Tech Day in the City of New York to recognize the contributions of women of color to the technology industry and to acknowledge the challenges they still face.

Items.Title: Resolution calling upon the United States Congress to pass, and the President to sign, H.J.Res.80/S.J.Res.38, establishing the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment
Items.AgendaNote: Adopted by the Committee on Women and Gender Equity
Items.MinutesNote:
Pursuant to Rule 8.60 of the Council, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) called for a voice vote.  Hearing those in favor, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) declared the Resolution to be adopted.



The following 5 Council Members formally noted their intent to vote negative on this item: 



Council Members Ariola, Morano, Vernikov, Paladino, and the Minority Leader (Council Member Carr).


Adopted by the Council by voice-vote.
Items.ActionText: This Resolution was Approved, by Council
Items.MatterName: Establishing the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment.

Items.Title: General Orders Calendar

Introduction & Reading of Bills
See Attached
Discussion of Resolutions
Resolutions
General Discussion
Extension of Remarks

INTRODUCTION AND READING OF BILLS

Pursuant to Section 33-a of the New York City Charter, the New York City Council may vote on the following items no earlier than 30 days from the date of this notice. The New York City Council reserves the right to vote without the notice required pursuant to Section 33-a on any proposed local laws that do not relate to the public safety operations of the New York City Police Department, the Fire Department or the Department of Correction.
Items.AgendaNote: INTRODUCTION AND READING OF BILLS - Pursuant to Section 33-a of the New York City Charter, the New York City Council may vote on the following items no earlier than 30 days from the date of this notice. The New York City Council reserves the right to vote without the notice required pursuant to Section 33-a on any proposed local laws that do not relate to the public safety operations of the New York City Police Department, the Fire Department or the Department of Correction.

Items.MinutesNote: The Majority Leader (Council Member Abreu) presiding as the President Pro Tempore.

The presence of a quorum was announced by the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu).

There were 50 Council Members marked present at this Stated Meeting.


There is one vacant seat on the Council pending the swearing-in of the certified winner of a Special Election to be held on April 28, 2026 in the 3rd Council District (Manhattan).
At this point the Speaker (Council Member Menin) made the following announcements:

Whereupon on motion of the Speaker (Council Member Menin), the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) adjourned these proceedings to meet again for the Stated Meeting of Thursday, April 16, 2026.

MICHAEL McSWEENEY, City Clerk
Clerk of the Council

The Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) put the question whether the Council would agree with and adopt such motion which was decided in the affirmative by the following vote:

At this point, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) declared that the aforementioned items were adopted and referred to the Committee on Land Use and to the appropriate Land Use Subcommittee.

Items.Title: Passed The Consent Agenda
Items.ActionText: A motion was made that these files be approved by consent vote.

Items.Title: Application number C 260071 HAM (1727 Amsterdam Avenue – Hill Top Apartments) submitted by the New York City Department of Housing Preservation and Development, pursuant to Article 16 of the General Municipal Law of New York State for the designation of an Urban Development Action Area and an Urban Development Action Area Project, and pursuant to Section 197-c of the New York City Charter for the disposition of such property to a developer to be selected by HPD, for property located at 1727 Amsterdam Avenue (Block 2060, Lot 1), Borough of Manhattan, Community District 9, Council District 7.
